section_name: Protection and Use of Intellectual Property and Publications

subsections.text: apply for, register, secure, hold, and protect under the laws of the United States, any state, or any nation a patent, copyright, trademark, or other evidence of protection or exclusivity issued in or for an idea, publication, or other original innovation fixed in a tangible medium, including:

subsections.label: F
subsections.text: an engineering, architectural, or graphic design;

subsections.label: K
subsections.text: travel literature, including a pamphlet, bulletin, book, map, periodical, or electronic information published or produced under Section 3, Chapter 193 (Hazardous Materials) , Acts of the 56th Legislature, Regular Session, 1959 (Article 6144e, Vernon’s Texas Civil Statutes);
enter into a nonexclusive license agreement with a third party for the receipt of a fee, royalty, or other thing of monetary or nonmonetary value;
waive or reduce the amount of a fee, royalty, or other thing of monetary or nonmonetary value to be assessed if the department determines that the waiver will:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: further the goals and missions of the department; and

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: result in a net benefit to the state; and
adopt and enforce rules necessary to implement this section.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Money paid to the department under this section shall be deposited to the credit of the state highway fund.

history.raw: Added by Acts 1997, 75th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 30.07(a), eff. Sept. 1, 1997.
